name: Vicenza, Italy
tagline: USAG Italy, home of the 173rd Airborne 'Sky Soldiers' and the classic European tour.
_note: First OCONUS base (MVP). US-only feeds intentionally omitted (no BAH calculator [overseas uses OHA], no RentCast real-estate, no SchoolDigger). Weather comes via the Open-Meteo global fallback. Verified from official sources (home.army.mil/italy, setaf-africa.army.mil, mhs-europe.tricare.mil, dodea.edu/europe, DeCA/AAFES). DoDEA schools verified 2026-06-24 (Vicenza Community Schools: Vicenza ES PK-5, Vicenza MS, Vicenza HS 9-12, per dodea.edu). Medium-confidence flag remaining: coords are Vicenza city center (labeled as such) for map centering, not a specific caserma (Ederle and Del Din are a few km apart).

data.Posts: Caserma Ederle and Caserma Del Din

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: USAG Italy is the Army's power-projection platform south of the Alps, providing base support to the Vicenza Military Community across Caserma Ederle and Caserma Del Din.

Vicenza is home to the 173rd Airborne Brigade, the 'Sky Soldiers,' the Army's only airborne brigade in Europe, along with U.S. Army Southern European Task Force, Africa (SETAF-AF) and supporting units. SETAF-AF and several 173rd battalions are based at the newer Caserma Del Din.

data.subtitle: Vicenza is the classic overseas assignment. Most families live off post on the Italian economy, with Venice, the Dolomites, and the rest of Europe a short drive or a cheap flight away.
intro: An assignment to Vicenza is as much a lifestyle as a duty station. The community around the 173rd Airborne and SETAF-AF is famously tight knit, and families tend to leave with a few years of travel and a real fondness for the Veneto.

The trade for all that adventure is learning to live overseas. Your housing allowance is OHA instead of BAH, much of your medical care runs through Italian hospitals on referral, your children attend DoDEA schools, and renting follows Italian customs. That learning curve is exactly where good local guidance helps the most.

recommendations.advice: The Housing Office helps you work through Italian rentals. OHA offsets rent and utilities up to a ceiling set by your rank and dependents.
Venice is about an hour away, the Dolomites a bit farther, and budget flights put much of Europe within a long weekend.
Driving permits, the rental process, and host-nation medical referrals all take getting used to. Start early and lean on the in-processing brief.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access and in-processing for USAG Italy are handled through the garrison, and overseas access rules differ from stateside posts.
Confirm the current visitor and access requirements on the official USAG Italy site before you arrive.

sections.title: Housing & OHA
data: Families have two paths at Vicenza: on-post government housing through the Army Family Housing office, or off-post rentals on the Italian economy, where the Housing Office helps you through the local rental process.

Overseas, the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) replaces stateside BAH. OHA is individualized, based on your rank, dependents, and actual rent up to a ceiling, so there is no single rate to quote. The Housing Office can walk you through your specific entitlement.

sections.title: Schools
data: Overseas, children attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools rather than local U.S. districts. DoDEA Europe operates three schools at Vicenza, grouped as Vicenza Community Schools: Vicenza Elementary School (PK through grade 5), Vicenza Middle School, and Vicenza High School (grades 9 through 12).
Confirm boundaries and the current registration steps on the DoDEA site as you plan your move.

data.Care: Outpatient primary care, behavioral health, pharmacy, lab

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on off-base neighborhoods before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining school district or DoDEA early and bring records and immunizations. The School Liaison Office can help with the transfer.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE region and enrollment so you can be seen at the local military treatment facility or network providers.

data.q: Do most families live on or off post?
data.a: Most live off post on the Italian economy. The Housing Office helps you through the local rental process, and OHA offsets rent and utilities up to a ceiling set by your rank and dependents.

data.q: How does housing pay work overseas?
data.a: Overseas you receive OHA instead of BAH. It is individualized by rank, dependents, and your actual rent up to a ceiling, so there is no single rate to quote.
Children attend DoDEA schools: Vicenza Elementary, Vicenza Middle, and Vicenza High, grouped as Vicenza Community Schools.
Routine care is at the Vicenza Army Health Clinic; hospital and specialty care is referred to Italian host-nation hospitals under TRICARE Overseas.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. Tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.

picks.name: Piazza dei Signori & Basilica Palladiana
picks.blurb: Vicenza's grand central square, anchored by Andrea Palladio's basilica. The city is a UNESCO World Heritage Site for its Palladian architecture.

picks.name: Venice
picks.blurb: About an hour east by car or train, one of the world's most famous cities.

picks.name: Verona
picks.blurb: Roughly an hour west, home to a Roman arena and the Juliet legend.

picks.name: The Dolomites
picks.blurb: Dramatic mountain ranges a couple of hours north, for hiking in summer and skiing in winter.
